name: Titanic Conspiracy
description: The Titanic conspiracy encompasses various theories suggesting the sinking of the RMS Titanic in 1912 was not merely an accident, but a deliberate act orchestrated for various nefarious purposes. Rooted in British societal anxieties of the time, these theories gained traction throughout the 20th and 21st centuries. A prominent theory involves the 'switch' claim, alleging the White Star Line swapped the Titanic with its older sister ship, the Olympic, to fraudulently claim insurance money after the Olympic had been damaged in a prior collision. Proponents cite superficial similarities between the ships, alleged subtle differences in photographs, and purported financial motivations of J.P. Morgan, who had financial interests in the White Star Line. Another theory focuses on the alleged murder of prominent opponents of the Federal Reserve, claiming they were deliberately eliminated by being booked on the Titanic. These theories often intertwine with anti-Semitic and anti-establishment sentiments. Mainstream historians and maritime experts dismiss these claims, citing extensive documentary evidence, ship blueprints, eyewitness testimonies, and forensic analysis that support the accidental sinking narrative. They point to the sheer logistical difficulty of swapping ships without detection, the lack of credible evidence of deliberate sabotage, and the misinterpretation or fabrication of evidence by conspiracy theorists. The official inquiries into the sinking, while subject to some criticism regarding their thoroughness, concluded the disaster was caused by excessive speed in icy waters and inadequate safety measures.
subtitle: More than just an accident?

claims.claim: The Titanic was switched with the Olympic.
claims.evidence: Superficial similarities, subtle photographic anomalies, White Star Line's financial troubles.
claims.criticism: Logistical impossibility of switching ships undetected, extensive documentation of Titanic's construction and features, misinterpretation of photographic evidence.

claims.claim: The sinking was orchestrated to eliminate opponents of the Federal Reserve.
claims.evidence: Prominent figures opposing the Federal Reserve were aboard the Titanic.
claims.criticism: Lack of concrete evidence linking their deaths to a deliberate plot, coincidental nature of their presence on the ship.

claims.claim: J.P. Morgan deliberately sank the ship for insurance money.
claims.evidence: J.P. Morgan's financial interests in the White Star Line, large insurance payout.
claims.criticism: Lack of evidence of sabotage, insurance payout would not have offset the cost of a new ship, Morgan cancelled his trip at the last minute due to illness.

keyFigures.name: Senan Molony
keyFigures.role: Promoter of the 'fire in coal bunker' theory.

keyFigures.name: Richard Davenport-Hines
keyFigures.role: Historian who critically examined the disaster in 'Titanic: A Night Remembered'

mythologyConnections: The Ship of Fools (allegory of human folly)

timeline.event: Renewed interest in Titanic conspiracy theories due to documentaries and online forums.

sources.author: Robin Gardiner
